This is my first game jam! Happy to hear any feedback or constructive criticism!

game.title: A World of My Own
game.short_text: We all live in our own worlds sometimes. What is real is just a matter of perspective.

details.title: A World of My Own
details.description:
Submission to the Brackeys Jam 2022.1 game jam. Theme: It Is NOT Real.

A World Of My Own
We all live in our own worlds. Sometimes, what is real and what is not is merely a matter of perspective. Opening your mind to different points of view is a necessity in life.
In this game, you do just that. Every object lives not only in your reality, but also in its own. Going into the reality of each object will allow you to solve problems that seem insurmountable if you restrict yourself to your reality.
Controls
Arrow keys / WASD - Move.Arrow key UP / W / Space - JumpE near an object - Enter its realityE when in an alternate reality - Return to your realityEsc - Pause/Unpause
The game contains 6 levels. In each you will need to change back and forth between different realities to reach the rabbit hole. Can you reach the end of all levels?
One of the levels presents an optional challenge in the form of a collectible crown that only those fully comfortable with reality switching will be able to reach!

Known Issues
If the loading bar gets stuck, refresh the page and try again. On Chrome, you may have more success if you Right Click -> Reload Frame. If all else fails you can also download the executable files.
The game sometimes halts for a few seconds during the title screen. Wait a few seconds and it should fix itself.
Thank you for playing!

Debuff / buff guide (don't read if you want to have a learning experience)
- Intimidated - Causes enemies to deal less damage, and allows you to heal when attacking them
- Dazed - Causes enemies to sometimes miss their attacks
- Paranoid - If you attack before the enemy, they flinch and lose their turn, however the enemy gains extra speed
- Stealth - Can stack. Increases speed slightly and multi-hit attacks will consume them to attack more times
- Drunk - deals slightly extra damage but has a chance to miss attacks
- Popular - Can stack. gains extra popularity

Making of the game
This is actually my first game jam submission ever. I was originally planning on working with a team until the theme was announced, and I had the idea to mix pixelated and none pixelated objects. I had learned how to do this awhile ago with the tutorial here. My inspiration for a lot of the game was pretty directly inspired by games like The Stanley Parable and Doki Doki Literature Club. The sound and music was also done by me, mostly in Ableton and MaxMSP. game.short_text: Sift through pieces of real and fake evidence before linking them together to solve the crime!

details.title: Red Herring
details.description:
Welcome Detective!
Congrats on your promotion! Now that you're a solo detective it's up to you to sift through evidence and link it together to solve mysteries and crimes in the local area. Be careful that you don't go down false lines of enquiry or fall for red herrings in this simple puzzle game. 
This game was made for the Brackeys 2022.1 game jam with the theme "This is not real"
How to Play
After listening to the call from your boss, click the document basket on your desk to pin evidence to the cork board. You can click on documents, photos and newspaper clippings to inspect them or click and drag to move them around the board. You can click and drag the thumbtacks to link the evidence together. 
Once the evidence is linked to form a valid conclusion your pencil will write on your investigative report. You can then submit this to your boss by clicking on the fax machine and then selecting submit.
Be careful! The conclusion you submit will determine the final outcome of the investigation and does not always lead to a happy ending!

game.title: Coin you see it?
game.short_text: You play as an antique store employee responsible for coin authenticity verification.

details.title: Coin you see it?
details.description:
Coin you see it?
About the Game 
Coin you see it? is a game prototype, where you play as an antique store employee that is responsible for verifying the authenticity of coins people are trying to sell to the store.
During your 8 AM to 7 PM shift, customers will come with the coins they want to sell. Besides the coins, you will obtain a document with the description of the coins you are verifying. The store will buy every coin you mark as real for the estimated price in the document. The coins will later be sold for a small profit. But make sure to inspect the coins closely! If you accidentally buy a fake coin, the store will sell it at a loss later on.
At the end of the day, you will see an overview of coins that were bought from the customers and coins that were sold by the store (these can be coins from the current day as well as from the previous days). You can also check the stock of the store - what coins were not yet sold.
You can use the store's profit to buy items at the equipment store. However, currently, only a sweet victory can be bought in the game.
Controls
Move your Mouse to look around
Press E to interact (coins, document, drawer)Hold LMB to rotate the coin (when inspecting)Hold RMB to look closer (not only when inspecting)Press ESC to cancel interaction or open pause menu

About Authors
Hey! We are John and Tom, and this was our first GameJam ever and the second game we made together. We hope you'll have at least a few minutes of fun with it, and feel free to give us your feedback!
If you are searching for new Game Dev communities, you can join our Discord server. It's very young, and this is the first time we invite the public.

game.title: Cinemare

details.title: Cinemare
details.description:
You're a ticket seller in a cinema.
Before the start of the film, you have to sell as many customers as possible the ticket they order. 
Unfortunately, ghosts constantly mingle in the queue and ask for tickets. Since ghost money is not real you have to scare them away with a crucifix.
Day 1 is easy, but the ghosts get harder to spot every night.The goal is to achieve the highest possible score. Feel free to post it in the comments below.

Controls:
Sell red tickets with "S", blue tickets with "A" and scare away ghosts with "D". You can also click on the buttons with the mouse.

This game was developed within a week for Brackeys Game Jam 7 with the theme "An end is a new beginning".

game.title: Station
game.short_text: Solve the mystery of this train station. Fortified & sealed off to protect its inhabitants from the monsters outside.

details.title: Station
details.description:
[UPDATE: Guide is now in the comments. Scroll to the bottom of this page to check on how to solve the puzzle you're stuck on. If you're having a problem that isn't there, let me know and we'll add it! Please do give us any and all feedback, it means a lot to hear from you all.]
Welcome to the station.
A 3D puzzle adventure that tells the story of a decaying mind, sealed away in a train station.
The game is all centred around a modified chess board, resembling the train station you find yourself in. Your task is to find the 5 pieces that need to be placed on the board, and find the 5 scraps of knowledge to help guide you on where to place them. Deduction and exploration are important parts of the game, so be sure to think carefully about all that the ticket master has to say to you, and keep a careful eye out for anything that might be of importance.
Known bugs.
A Jam game wouldn't be complete without a variety of bugs popping up post-publication. Here are some of ours!
DO NOT CLICK THE STACK OF CARDS TWICE BEFORE DOING THE PUZZLE. Doing so will lock the puzzle and make it impossible to complete the game! (sorry)Cursor is sometimes stuck in the eye position. Can be fixed by walking up to a wall and back or by looking at the ground. (Raycasting problem) Multiple playthroughs result in the clock audio doing funky things (But honestly if you're playing the game multiple times I'm flattered.)
The architects of the station.
Station is a game made by Ethan Kessler.
Landscape art by InnerWindowStudios
2D Sprites by Shotgunsexual
Sound design and music by Jhaych

Leave us a comment so we can play your game as well! Plus, we love feedback!
